description: Generic mapping of US state privacy laws (CCPA, VCDPA, CTDPA, UCPA, CPA) to NIST Cybersecurity Framework

source.disclaimer: These mappings provide general guidance for state privacy law compliance using NIST CSF. Individual state laws have variations. Organizations should consult state-specific requirements and legal counsel. This mapping applies to: California CCPA/CPRA, Virginia VCDPA, Connecticut CTDPA, Utah UCPA, Colorado CPA.
source.methodology: Mappings based on common privacy law requirements: consumer rights, data protection, transparency, accountability. Focuses on technical and organizational measures to support compliance.
source.note: This is a generic mapping applicable to multiple state privacy laws. For regulation-specific mappings, use individual regulation IDs (CCPA, VCDPA, etc.)

mappings.control_name: Resources are prioritized based on classification, criticality, and business value
mappings.rationale: Data classification supports right to know / access requests

mappings.control_name: Data is destroyed according to policy
Priorities for organizational mission, objectives, and activities are established and communicated
mappings.rationale: Collect only adequate, relevant, and necessary data

mappings.control_name: Identities and credentials are issued, managed, verified, revoked, and audited
mappings.rationale: Security practices to protect personal data

mappings.control_name: Detected events are analyzed to understand attack targets and methods
Incidents are reported consistent with established criteria
mappings.rationale: CCPA private right of action for data breaches

mappings.control_name: Suppliers and third-party partners are identified, prioritized, and assessed
Asset vulnerabilities are identified and documented
mappings.rationale: Data protection assessments for high-risk processing

mappings.control_name: Removable media is protected and its use restricted
mappings.rationale: Tangentially related to data portability (obtaining copy in usable format)

mappings.control_name: Cybersecurity is included in human resources practices
mappings.rationale: Privacy notice and transparency requirements - staff training
